# spark-html

> Spark is a no-build reactive HTML framework: the file you write is the file
> that runs. No compiler, no virtual DOM, no build step for the user. HTML +
> `{expr}` + a handful of directives + stores — one runtime (~16 KB gzip, 0
> deps) that works the same as a plain client app, a build-time-prerendered
> static site, or a full-stack server-rendered app via the spark-ssr sibling
> package (inferred database, REST API, auth, jobs — the HTML template is the
> spec). A page written for one mode runs in the other two by relocation, not
> rewrite.

Core ideas worth knowing before reading further: components are plain `.html`
files (markup + `<script>` + `<style>`, scoped by default); reactivity is
plain `let`/assignment — no signals, no hooks, no dependency arrays; `<spark-ssr>`
blocks declare data (SQL, a table, a URL, a markdown glob, or a JS module) and
the server infers schema, REST endpoints, and hydration from how the template
reads it.

## Docs

- [Full documentation](https://spark-html.dev/docs): concept, installation,
  components, template syntax (if/else-if/else, `<template await>`, literal
  braces), slots, reactive statements & lifecycle, JS imports, props, stores
  (incl. derived), forms, async data, JavaScript API, dev server & build,
  editor support, how it works, performance, debugging, error handling,
  prerender for SEO, and the full spark-ssr section (below).
- [spark-ssr guide](https://spark-html.dev/docs#ssr): the template is the
  backend, any data source (SQL/URL/markdown/module), ambient helpers
  (`api_create`/`api_update`/`api_delete`/`refresh`/`navigate` — ambient
  client-side helpers a hydrating page gets with zero imports; `navigate`
  intercepts same-page `<a href="?…">` clicks and swaps data via
  `history.pushState` + `refresh()` instead of a full reload), batteries
  included (auth, guards, no-JS forms, relations, jobs & mail, sitemap/robots/OpenAPI),
  fast by default (precompiled render, response cache, streaming).
- [spark-ssr full guide](https://spark-html.dev/ssr): quick start, what gets
  inferred, config, routing & layouts, data sources, a full Pinterest-style
  SQL-driven walkthrough, a markdown/module (no-database) walkthrough,
  interactive pages, auth & sessions, forms without JavaScript, the template
  as schema, background jobs & mail, performance, security, deploy.
- [Ecosystem](https://spark-html.dev/docs#ecosystem): every sibling package
  and what it does — spark-html-bun (dev server/bundler), spark-html-router,
  spark-html-theme, spark-html-head, spark-html-motion, spark-html-devtools,
  spark-html-query, spark-html-persist, spark-html-websocket, spark-prerender,
  spark-ssr, spark-html-image, spark-html-font, spark-html-manifest,
  spark-html-offline, spark-html-sri, create-spark-html-app,
  prettier-plugin-spark, spark-html-language-server.
- [Limitations](https://spark-html.dev/docs#limits): the honestly-documented
  edges of the framework — read before assuming a gap is a bug.

## Try it

- [Playground](https://spark-html.dev/playground): live in-browser editor,
  no install.
- [Tutorials](https://spark-html.dev/tutorials): guided walkthroughs.
- [Showcase](https://spark-html.dev/showcase): real apps built with Spark.

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/wilkinnovo/spark-html): monorepo — all
  packages, examples (basic, jsimports, no-build, pinterest, spark-chat,
  spark-share, tabtube), and the website itself.
- [npm: spark-html](https://www.npmjs.com/package/spark-html) — the core
  runtime. Sibling packages are `spark-html-<name>` or `spark-<name>` on npm;
  see Ecosystem above for the full list.

## Optional

- [og.png](https://spark-html.dev/og.png): social preview image, not
  documentation content.
